subject: DevOps
title: Black Box vs White Box Monitoring
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Black Box vs White Box Monitoring.

hook.text: A user sees a 500 error. Inside, the server logs show nothing. Two views, two truths.

buildup.text: Black box monitoring tests from outside, like a user. White box reads internal signals.

discovery.text: Black box finds user-facing failures. White box reveals internal causes and bottlenecks.

twist.text: Black box shows what users see. White box shows why. You need both perspectives.

climax.text: Combine synthetic checks (black box) with internal metrics (white box) for full coverage.

punchline.text: Outside and inside views together. Full picture.

quiz.question: What does black box monitoring test?
options: The system from the user's external perspective
Source code quality and test coverage
